reviews.review_msg: shocked to see the ratings were low for Weiran, had her for two classes and shes quite a good professor. Shes concise, she knows what shes doing, doesnt freeze up when a student asks her a question and lots of bonus/chances to improve grade. I would take her for upper div courses for sure

Weiran is very approachable and passionate about the subject. It's a hard course but if you go to office hours and pay attention in class Its very doable. Quizzes are hard but lots of homework and quiz retakes for partial marks.

Weiran is an impressive prof who lectures without notes, unlike most profs, and explains concepts clearly. The course is about 8/10 in difficulty—tough if you struggled with Math 251. Attend every class since notes aren't posted. Quizzes and tutorials can be tricky, so study ahead. Stay on top of things! Overall, goated prof!

shes the human wolfram alpha with logic and doesnt make mistakes like wolfram sometimes does, does the lecture off the top of her head and doesnt miss anything from the textbook. lots of opportunities to get bonus marks, really clear lectures. If you have weiran for math consider that a W.  math 252 in general though is a big workload.
Amazing professor, exams can be hard, but she's passionate and engaging as a lecturer.

some of the assignments and quizzes can be on the harder side, however you can tell she knows her stuff. She walks in with just chalk and then does the whole divergence theorem, applications, examples, and how it correlates to previous stuff off the top of her head w no errors. Really solid lecturer compared to other math profs

as a lecturer shes really good and any question you ask she has an answer whereas some profs would fumble. She gives lots of opportunity for extra credit and her notes are clear. I would just say the quizzes are on the harder side and the midterms are fair but a tad difficult. DO AS MUCH PRACTICE POSSIBLE

I had weiran for calc 3 and 4 and honestly shes a really solid professor, clear grading outcomes, lectures well and provides alternative ways to explain concepts you can tell she knows her stuff. I would say quizzes are really difficult and she expects more than whats taught in class sometimes but shes good tbh
Pretty sure shes a spy yikes
Fantastic lecturer. Truly an expert

shes ok at teaching, midterms are somewhat reasonable but the weekly quizzes are actually horrendous. The quizzes are always filled with some random really difficult question we havent seen. if you HAD to take this prof sure, if you can take another maybe go with the other prof
Quizzes are hard!!! Run away!!!
So bad!!!!! Horrible!!!
doesn't post notes. literally no reason not to, just gonna make students show up to class sick
Lots of self learning
Doesn't post lecture note and her lectures were very confusing

this is a review for math 320. dr sun is great. shes extremely helpful in office hours, so i highly encourage you to go. she doesn't post notes. shes always willing to stop and take the time to iron out confusion in lectures. exams are heavily based on homework/tutorial questions. lectures can sometimes be hard to follow for harder topics.

Dr. Sun's intelligence is inspiring, her lectures are captivating, and she is caring. Her assignments are tough sometimes, but she's always accessible via email, during office hours, or after class. 418 is a tricky course, so be ready to review MATH251&MATH310. She doesn't post lecture notes, but this was an incentive to pay attention in class.

Professor Sun is easily the worst professor I have ever had in my entire SFU career. She gives insanely hard exams and has a very firm and cold demeanour. Makes course content harder than it has to be and purposely tries to confuse the learner. I am writing this inform you to drop the course you have with her for your own well being.

She is very helpful, gives you very clear answer to your questions. But make sure you read the textbook, because analytics is not an easy thing to learn AT ALL! You'd better roughly review the sections that she's about to teach, and carefully read textbooks after class (I think it's true for most MATH courses, many reviews didn't realize it)
